messages.content: Always use code blocks with the appropriate language tags. If asked for a table always format it using Markdown syntax.

To delete the `windows.old` files on your Windows system, you can use the Disk Cleanup utility. Here's how you can do it:

```bash
cleanmgr
```

Select the drive where Windows is installed (usually C: drive) and click OK. Then, select the "Previous Windows installations" option in the Disk Cleanup window and click OK to delete the `windows.old` files.

Alternatively, you can also use the `StorageSense` feature in Windows 10 to delete the `windows.old` folder. Here's how you can do it:

1. Go to Settings > System > Storage.
2. Turn on the Storage Sense feature.
3. Click on "Configure Storage Sense or run it now."
4. Under "Free up space now," click on the "Clean now" button.

This will automatically delete temporary files and the `windows.old` folder from your system.
